plan_adherence_notes: All 11 plan steps were executed. Steps 1-3 (file setup, mocking, fixtures) match exactly. Steps 4-10 (test cases) all implemented. Step 11 (run vitest) confirmed 13/13 pass. The implementation slightly exceeded the plan: step 5 used raw number matching instead of toLocaleString() (acceptable since fixture values < 1000), step 8 expanded from one formatBytes test to three boundary tests (512B, 2.0KB, 1.0MB), and step 10 added an inverse test for error_count=0. The only minor deviation: step 5 planned to test 'states via toLocaleString' but the test checks clique summary stats (num_cliques, max_clique_size, junction_tree_width) rather than individual clique states values — this is acceptable since the clique cards are rendered and states appear via CliqueCard sub-component. Decision d4 (use small fixture values) was correctly followed.
missed_questions: How does the component behave when data is null/undefined after loading (data=undefined, isLoading=false)? The component returns null in this case but no test covers it.
Should the CliqueCard sub-component rendering be tested (variable tags, state bar colors based on barPct thresholds)? The plan omitted CliqueCard-specific tests.
Does the engine info banner render correctly (mode/backend/version/precision/compile_id/source_state_hash)? No test covers this section.
reason: All 11 plan steps were executed faithfully. 13 tests pass covering loading state, clique tree summary stats, inference diagnostics (requests, errors, cache hit rate, latency percentiles), all 3 optional fields (compile_time_ms, memory_bytes with 3 formatBytes boundary cases, last_updated), and error_count danger color styling with both positive and negative cases. Research correctly identified the vi.mock pattern, renderWithProviders usage, formatBytes non-export status, and toLocaleString handling strategy — all reflected in the implementation. The missed questions (empty data state, CliqueCard details, engine banner) are minor gaps that don't affect the core task goal of testing JunctionTreePanel's key behaviors.
